title: euphorbia engleri
intro: Euphorbia engleri, also known as the finger tree or finger plant, is a species of succulent plant in the Euphorbiaceae family. It is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce fever. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, and its health benefits are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. Herbal preparations of Euphorbia engleri are often made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the plant material for use in capsules or tinctures.
